title: LLMRouter — promote Cerebras, add tier param, TPD-budget-aware brownout
current_state: core/llm_router.py routes by availability only — brownout (5min cooldown on failure) + first-healthy. No cost awareness, no task-fit routing, no TPD-budget guard. Cerebras (the actual primary for normie post-S-053) bypasses LLMRouter entirely: instantiated as raw OpenAI client in pi_agent.py:140-150, with hand-rolled Cerebras→Groq failover inside _respond_normie. Root mode failover goes through LLMRouter (Claude→Groq→Gemini). Two failover code paths. S-053's better_future_fix calls out the gap explicitly.
structural_problem: Two failover code paths means brownout-TTL bugs fixed in LLMRouter silently regress normie. Pi makes ~10x more LLM calls in Phase 7 autonomy than chat, mostly to Claude by default — cost grows unbounded without per-task routing. Daily TPD-limit hits (Groq ~100k, observed S-053) cause silent provider failures with no preemptive routing-around.
target_state: All LLM calls flow through LLMRouter.chat(messages, system, tools, max_tokens, tier='cheap'|'balanced'|'premium'). Cerebras is a full CerebrasProvider in core/providers/cerebras.py. Per-tier provider preference lists. core/cost_tracker.py tracks per-provider daily token usage; brownout at 90% TPD utilization. Default tier matrix: normie=cheap, root code-edits=premium, root tool-call planning=balanced, distillation=cheap, briefing aggregation=cheap.
files_affected: core/providers/cerebras.py (promote from raw client to full CerebrasProvider matching AnthropicProvider/GroqProvider shape)
core/llm_router.py (add tier param; add per-tier provider preference; add TPD-budget brownout check before _is_browned_out)
core/cost_tracker.py (add per-provider daily token tracking table or query)
pi_agent.py (remove self.cerebras direct init; remove _respond_normie hand-rolled failover; call LLMRouter with tier='cheap' for normie)
memory/pipeline.py (call LLMRouter with tier='cheap' for distillation instead of direct groq_client + anthropic_client)
tools/tools_briefing.py (call LLMRouter with tier='cheap' for aggregation)
success_criteria: All LLM calls observably go through LLMRouter.chat — pi_agent.py has no remaining direct anthropic/groq/cerebras client calls
TPD-budget brownout fires at 90% utilization on any provider, observable via daemon stats action
Test: mock Cerebras to report 91% TPD → next call routes to next-cheap provider automatically
Tier matrix documented in PI.md §6 or core/llm_router.py docstring
risk_notes: Touches the hot path for every LLM call. Mitigation: ship CerebrasProvider first (pure addition); migrate normie path next as separate commit; add tier+TPD third. Each step independently revertable.
migration_plan: 3. Register Cerebras with LLMRouter in pi_agent.py __init__; remove self.cerebras direct client
4. Add tier param to LLMRouter.chat with provider preference lists per tier; default tier='balanced' preserves current behavior
5. Migrate _respond_normie to call router.chat(..., tier='cheap'); remove hand-rolled Cerebras→Groq fallback
6. Migrate memory/pipeline.py::distill_session to call router with tier='cheap'
7. Extend core/cost_tracker.py with per-provider daily_tokens table or rolling query
8. Add TPD-budget brownout check in LLMRouter._is_browned_out: if tokens_today / provider.daily_limit > 0.9, mark browned out
9. Add test simulating TPD saturation and verifying routing skips saturated providers
10. Append SOLUTIONS.jsonl with note that S-053's better_future_fix is now resolved
